title: Harden ME3 Core owner auth and onboarding
description: Replace the first-boot localStorage auth shim with a simple, secure owner-auth flow for OSS installs. Bootstrap should remain provider-free and easy to run locally, but successful bootstrap/login must establish a server-verified owner session instead of trusting client-only state.
design: Suggested shape: POST /api/admin/bootstrap validates ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_CODE, upserts owner_profile, and issues an httpOnly signed session cookie using JWT_SECRET. Add /api/auth/me and /api/auth/logout for session hydration/logout. Protect owner-only Worker API routes with session verification. Keep Postmark/email providers optional and outside auth. Web login should clearly present first-run bootstrap and existing-owner unlock states without requiring email delivery.
acceptance_criteria:
- First-run setup works without Postmark, OAuth, or any email provider.
- Successful bootstrap creates/updates the owner profile and sets an httpOnly signed session cookie.
- Refreshing the app hydrates auth from /api/auth/me, not localStorage.
- Logout clears the server session cookie and client state.
- Owner-only API routes reject unauthenticated requests.
- README documents the simple local onboarding flow and required secrets.
- Web and worker tests cover bootstrap success/failure, session hydration, logout, and protected route rejection.
close_reason: Implemented server-verified owner auth with httpOnly session cookie, /api/auth/me hydration, logout, protected assistant route, docs, tests, and local boot verification.
